Identity.History: Chandragupta II ruled the Gupta Empire around 375–415 AD and presided over one of its greatest periods. Son of Samudragupta, he expanded Gupta power westward by defeating the Western Indo-Scythian Kshatrapas, gaining control of Gujarat and access to rich maritime trade. His reign saw prosperity, artistic achievement, and courtly culture, later associated with India’s “Golden Age.” He used the title Vikramaditya, “Sun of Valor,” and strengthened Gupta prestige through conquest, diplomacy, and patronage.
